sentence_reviews.text: Edge and on-device deployment runs models near sensors or users under strict power, memory, latency, connectivity, privacy, and thermal constraints.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Quantization and accelerator references support efficiency constraints; the specific edge/on-device constraint list is not directly sourced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Add an edge ML deployment source or narrow to quantization/accelerator constraints.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment is to connect Quantized inference & compressed deployment, GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators to downstream capabilities including Robotics & embodied AI, Neuromorphic, sensor & edge intelligence.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The sources support quantization and accelerators; downstream robotics and neuromorphic links are atlas-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible graph synthesis, not directly verified.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Accelerator references support deployed compute generally, but not robotics/embodied AI specifically.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Neuromorphic, sensor & edge intelligence

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Quantized inference & compressed deployment, GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support some efficiency, numerical, hardware, and risk concerns; the complete generic limitation sentence is template-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency list is graph-derived; security/versioning/observability need direct evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: Quantized inference is central to constrained deployment.
Accelerators are a core deployment substrate.
No adequate packet citation supports SNN as a common dependency here.
Verification is relevant to safety-critical edge deployments but not shown as a common operational dependency.
Risk-management evidence is broad, not edge-specific.
Compression methods are plausible optional enablers for constrained deployment.
